title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. and Iran Trade Strikes, Further Threatening Negotiations
summary: The U.S. military said Iran had launched a ballistic missile toward Kuwait. Iran’s Revolutionary Guards said it had targeted an unspecified U.S. base in retaliation for earlier strikes.

title: The Mideast Is Baffled by Trump’s Call to Expand Abraham Accords
summary: The president said more countries should be required to recognize Israel as part of a deal to end the war with Iran. Analysts say the chances of that happening are close to zero.

title: Why Texas Students Are Being Tackled and Tasered, and Trump’s Latest Target for Retribution
summary: Plus, inside the hunt for cheap gas.

title: Justice Dept. Is Said to Open Criminal Inquiry of E. Jean Carroll, Who Accused Trump of Rape
summary: Ms. Carroll, who prevailed in a civil trial after accusing President Trump of sexual abuse, is the latest target in a Justice Department campaign going after his perceived enemies.

title: These Are Some of the Trump Justice Dept.’s Targets
summary: When he returned to office, President Trump vowed revenge against those he said had challenged or defied him. Here’s an incomplete list of the people his Justice Department has targeted.

title: ‘Up for Grabs’: Can Democrats Sway Young Men Who Have Soured on Trump?
summary: Many Gen Z men who voted for the president are dismayed by his time in office. But they are not hearing an appealing pitch from Democrats, either: “Both parties kind of get it wrong.”

title: James Talarico Opens Texas Senate Campaign Against Ken Paxton With Houston Rally
summary: Personal attacks and a packed campaign rally set the tone for what is likely to be a rancorous contest in Texas for U.S. Senate between James Talarico and Ken Paxton.

title: Judge Declines, for Now, to Block Mail-In Voting Changes Ordered by Trump
summary: The ruling allowed the Trump administration to continue to pursue changes that include enlisting the Postal Service in checking voters against a national database.

title: The Nonfiction Everyone Will Be Reading This Summer
summary: Memoirs, histories, true crime, investigations and much more.

title: The Novels Everyone Will Be Reading This Summer
summary: New fiction from Maggie O’Farrell, Ann Patchett, Colson Whitehead, Silvia Moreno-Garcia and much more.

title: Former Judges Urge Inquiry Into Deal Trump Struck With I.R.S.
summary: The motion was particularly significant because it asked the judge overseeing the initial suit against the I.R.S. to examine the terms of the deal.

title: What is Trump’s $1.776 Billion ‘Anti-Weaponization’ Fund?
summary: The Trump administration is creating a $1.8 billion fund to compensate people it says were wronged by the federal government, a group that could be largely made up of the president’s allies.

title: Guatemala Agrees to Joint Strikes With U.S. Against Drug Gangs
summary: The deal is part of a broader effort by the Trump administration to press Latin American countries to agree to joint operations inside their borders.

title: Cuba Loses Its Chance at Fuel After Russian Tanker Changes Route
summary: A Russian tanker seemingly en route to Cuba with a lifeline of fuel has changed direction, a painful development for the island suffering under a U.S. oil blockade.

title: ‘Vibrant as Ever’: Harvard’s Jewish Life Thrives as Trump Investigates
summary: Trump officials have focused on episodes during protests over the war in Gaza at Harvard and other schools. Jews on campus describe a changed atmosphere.

title: In Argentina, U.S. Tech Billionaire Peter Thiel Finds An Escape
summary: The billionaire’s new roots in Argentina are said to be partly motivated by concerns about the future of the United States and shared beliefs with Argentina’s right-wing leader.

title: Happy, Bronx Zoo Elephant at Center of Animal Rights Case, Is Dead at 55
summary: Activists had sued in a bid to secure her the fundamental human right to bodily liberty. Zoo officials said she was well cared for and called the lawsuit frivolous.

title: Happy the Elephant Hasn’t Been Seen in Months. What’s Going On?
summary: The star attraction of the Bronx Zoo hasn’t been seen since July. The zoo says she’s fine. Animal rights activists are not so sure.

title: Online Scams Have Evolved in the A.I. Era. Here’s What to Do.
summary: A criminal could be masquerading as a celebrity, web store or family member asking for your money. Detecting scams requires a new approach.

title: Streaming TV Bundles Grow in Popularity
summary: Companies and their customers are embracing bundled streaming packages — but for different reasons.

title: The Google Insider Trading Case Hits Polymarket
summary: Charges against a Google employee who bet on Polymarket have raised concerns that the issue may dent momentum in the fast-growing sector.

title: Bare-Knuckle Tactics and Brash Promises: How Howard Lutnick Operates
summary: Before becoming U.S. commerce secretary, Mr. Lutnick controlled 818 companies. A review of their dealings sheds light on his freewheeling first year in government.

title: 4 Takeaways From The Times’s Reporting on Howard Lutnick
summary: The New York Times examined the dealings of 818 companies controlled by the U.S. commerce secretary, documenting a history of bare-knuckle tactics that extended into his time in government.

title: Not Every Language Has Spelling Bees. Here’s Why English Is Perfect for Them.
summary: English’s hybrid history, silent letters and overcomplicated rules are what make the competition fierce.

title: How to Watch the Scripps National Spelling Bee
summary: Nearly 250 contestants from around the nation and the world traveled to Washington for a chance at glory and a $50,000 cash prize.

title: Big Swells of Rancor Are Capsizing America
summary: Texas just got a lot more interesting.

title: What Cubans Really Think About Trump
summary: A filmmaker asks residents of the embattled island what they would say to the U.S. president.

title: A Violent Education
summary: We look at an extraordinary investigation into police officers in Texas schools.

title: Jimmy Kimmel Can Hardly Believe the Reality of L.A.’s Mayoral Race
summary: “Mayor should not be your first job,” the host said of the former reality show star and mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt, who is polling in second place.

title: Scientists Find a Potential Cure for Chronic Hepatitis B
summary: Most people recover from the infection, but it poses great risks for those who don’t. A new drug may cure 1 in 5 of these patients.

title: Mamdani Vowed to Back Espaillat. Now He May Endorse a D.S.A. Challenger.
summary: The mayor is seriously considering getting behind Darializa Avila Chevalier, a democratic socialist challenging Representative Adriano Espaillat in an Upper Manhattan House primary.

title: What Plunging Pork Prices Say About China’s Economy
summary: A key measure of inflation in China, they hit a 16-year low, driven by anemic consumer spending and an oversupply of hogs.

title: The Real Story Behind “Pressure,” the Film About the D-Day Weather Forecast
summary: The new film “Pressure” tells the story of the fateful D-Day weather forecast. Here’s what it got right and wrong from the historical record.
